content:
I tap the edge of the notebook with a blunt fingernail. The sound is a dry, percussive snap that only I can truly feel, a vibration traveling up my arm to remind me that I am made of matter. 

Zhang’s hands are a provocation. They are too light, too unburdened by the grit of Stockton Street. My own hands are mapped with the stains of damp earth and the sticky residue of overripe fruit, a history w
